steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Hawaii's literature has a rich history brought to international prominence by writers like Pukui. The blank describes what Eric Chock has done to “his place in that literary tradition” by producing acclaimed works. The blank must mean he has firmly established or secured his place.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'extricated': means removed or freed from. Chock would be removing himself from the tradition — the opposite of what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice B — 'contemplated': means thought about. Producing acclaimed works is an action, not merely thinking about a place. ✗
  • Choice C — 'subverted': means undermined or overthrown. The passage presents Chock as continuing the tradition, not working against it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'solidified': means made firm or secure. Producing acclaimed works would cement Chock's place in a tradition he is building on. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
